name: WeChat MCP Server
what_it_does: WeChat MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with WeChat messaging platform — sending messages to contacts and groups, reading received messages, accessing WeChat contact lists, and integrating WeChat into agent-driven communication workflows for Chinese market messaging and social platform automation.
use_cases: Sending automated WeChat messages from customer service agents
Reading and responding to WeChat messages from communication agents
Managing WeChat contacts and groups from CRM integration agents
Monitoring WeChat for keywords or notifications from surveillance agents
Building WeChat chatbots integrated with AI from conversational agents
Automating WeChat business account responses from marketing agents
not_for: Bulk marketing message spam (violates WeChat ToS)
Teams not targeting Chinese users (WeChat is predominantly China-market)
Enterprise WeChat (WeCom/企业微信) — requires different API
best_when: An agent needs to send or receive WeChat messages for Chinese market communication — particularly for customer service, notifications, or social automation targeting WeChat users.
avoid_when: You're targeting non-Chinese audiences (use WhatsApp, Telegram, or Slack MCPs), or need enterprise/business WeChat features (use WeCom API instead).

auth.notes: WeChat account login required — typically uses QR code scan to authenticate via WeChat Web protocol. Unofficial WeChat API access. Account may be restricted by WeChat for automation.

pricing.free_tier_limits: WeChat messaging is free; MCP server is free and open source
pricing.notes: WeChat personal accounts are free. MCP server is free and open source. Uses unofficial WeChat Web protocol — no official API costs but ToS risks.

performance.rate_limits: WeChat informal limits — aggressive automation may trigger account restrictions

known_agent_gotchas: Uses unofficial WeChat Web protocol — Tencent may break this at any time with no warning
WeChat account may be temporarily or permanently banned for automation violations
QR code authentication requires human scan to initialize — not headless-friendly
Session may expire frequently requiring re-authentication
All data routed through Chinese servers — sensitive data should not be sent via WeChat
Community MCP with minimal documentation — expect significant setup friction
agent_readiness.security_notes: CRITICAL: Unofficial WeChat API. All data passes through Tencent/Chinese servers. No privacy guarantees. Account ban risk. Do not send sensitive data. Use for low-stakes automation only.

security_score_components.security_notes: Unofficial API. China data routing. Account ban risk. No sensitive data. ToS violation risk.
